title: Bee Removal Upper East Side | $350-$550 | Best Pest Control NYC
metaDescription: Professional bee removal services in Upper East Side, Manhattan. Safe, humane bee extraction from $350-$550. Licensed experts serving UES homes & buildings. Call today!
intro: The Upper East Side's elegant brownstones, pre-war buildings, and tree-lined streets provide ideal conditions for bee colonies to establish nests in chimneys, eaves, and wall voids. Our professional bee removal services protect Upper East Side residents and their historic properties from unwanted bee infestations. We offer safe, humane bee extraction starting at $350-$550, with same-day service available throughout the UES.
whyUs: Best Pest Control NYC specializes in bee removal for Upper East Side's unique architectural landscape, from Carnegie Hill co-ops to Yorkville townhouses. Our licensed technicians understand the preservation requirements of the neighborhood's historic buildings and use gentle extraction methods that protect both the property and the bees whenever possible.
localContext: The Upper East Side's proximity to Central Park and abundance of flowering gardens along Fifth Avenue and Park Avenue attracts honeybees and bumblebees seeking pollen sources. The neighborhood's classic pre-war apartment buildings with decorative cornices and older townhouses with original masonry often develop bee entry points that require professional assessment and removal.

faqs.question: How much does bee removal cost in the Upper East Side?
faqs.answer: Bee removal in the Upper East Side typically costs between $350-$550, depending on the colony size, location accessibility, and whether structural work is needed. Pre-war buildings may require additional care due to their historic nature, which can affect pricing.

faqs.question: Do you offer same-day bee removal service in the UES?
faqs.answer: Yes, we provide same-day bee removal service throughout the Upper East Side for urgent situations. We understand that bee swarms near building entrances or outdoor dining areas require immediate attention, especially during peak pedestrian hours.

faqs.question: Can bees damage my Upper East Side brownstone or co-op building?
faqs.answer: Yes, established bee colonies can cause structural damage over time. Honeybees produce wax and honey that can stain walls, attract other pests, and create moisture problems. In historic UES buildings, prompt removal prevents damage to original woodwork, masonry, and architectural details.

faqs.question: What should I do if I find a bee nest near my Upper East Side property?
faqs.answer: Do not attempt to remove the nest yourself. Keep residents, children, and pets away from the area and contact us immediately. We'll dispatch a licensed technician to your Upper East Side location to safely assess and remove the colony, often relocating it when possible rather than exterminating.
